overview: This is a Mountain Bluebird (Sialia currucoides), recognized by its vibrant turquoise-blue plumage and white underbelly. The species is native to western North America and is the official state bird of both Idaho and Nevada. These birds typically inhabit open, high-elevation habitats and often hunt insects by hovering or pouncing from elevated perches. This is a Mountain Bluebird (Sialia currucoides), recognized by its vibrant turquoise-blue plumage and white underbelly.
The species is native to western North America and is the official state bird of both Idaho and Nevada.
These birds typically inhabit open, high-elevation habitats and often hunt insects by hovering or pouncing from elevated perches.
